questions.question: Which role is authorized to install workspace-wide security and compliance integrations in Notion?

options.text: Any workspace member with a verified domain
options.explanation: Only Enterprise workspace owners can install these integrations, not all members with a domain.
According to the prerequisites, only a workspace owner can configure security and compliance integrations.
Admin privileges are required in the partner tool, but only workspace owners can install the integrations.
Integrations are the tools being installed, not the users installing them.

questions.question: What is the primary function of integrating with a Data Loss Prevention (DLP) solution?

options.text: To automatically backup data to the cloud
options.explanation: While backups may happen, the primary function is detecting sensitive data and taking remediation actions.

options.text: To detect the use of sensitive data and take automated action to remediate breaches
options.explanation: DLP integrations help detect sensitive data and take automated action like alerting, redacting, or restricting access.

options.text: To compress files for faster loading
options.explanation: File compression is not a function of DLP integrations.
DLP focuses on data content, not user authentication methods.

questions.question: How many Datadog instances can be connected to a single Notion workspace?

options.explanation: There is a limitation regarding the number of instances that can be connected.
The documentation notes that one Datadog instance can only be connected to one workspace at most.
The limitation is tied to the workspace, not individual users.
There is no mention of a total limit of three; the limit is per workspace.

questions.question: Which SIEM event type corresponds to a user successfully logging into their account?

options.explanation: user.logout refers to a user logging out of an account, not logging in.
The event type 'user.login' is specifically defined as a user logging into an account.
user.deleted refers to when a user account is deleted.
This event tracks updates to Multi-Factor Authentication settings, not the initial login.

questions.question: When integrating with Drata, which of the following is a restriction regarding page types?

options.explanation: You should avoid using database blocks for the best experience, but the restriction is on private pages.

options.text: You cannot sync private Notion pages
options.explanation: The documentation explicitly states that you can't sync any private Notion pages to Drata.
Public pages can be synced; private pages are restricted.

options.text: You cannot sync pages with external guests
options.explanation: External guests are not the restriction; private page visibility is.

questions.question: Which page audience describes a page that is shared with guests outside the workspace and/or with an integration bot?

options.explanation: Internal means shared with members of the workspace only.
External describes a page shared with guests outside the workspace and/or integration bots.
Public means shared to the web.
Private means not shared with other users.

questions.question: For the Panther integration, what specific Header Name is associated with the Secret Key Value?

options.explanation: The token is the value, but the header name is different.
The Header Name associated with the Secret Key Value is locked to 'x-notion-signature'.
The correct header name is x-notion-signature.
The correct header name is x-notion-signature.

questions.question: How does Notion log page content edits in the SIEM platform?

options.explanation: Events are consolidated, not logged per individual block.

options.text: One event every minute while edits are occurring
options.explanation: Content edit events are consolidated into one event every minute while edits are occurring.

options.text: No events are logged for page content edits
options.explanation: Page content edits are logged as 'page.content_edited' events.
This describes page creation, not content editing.

questions.question: What access do SIEM integrations have within the workspace?

options.text: Access to view all member email addresses
options.explanation: SIEMs receive logs, they don't necessarily have direct access to member lists in that way.

options.text: Receive event logs on all workspace activity
options.explanation: SIEM integrations are authorized to receive event logs on all workspace activity.
SIEM integrations only receive data, they do not have edit permissions.

options.text: Delete permissions for the entire workspace
options.explanation: SIEM integrations only receive logs, they do not have deletion permissions.

questions.question: Which of the following Splunk instance types is currently supported for integration?

options.explanation: Splunk On-Prem is not supported; only Cloud or Enterprise licenses are supported.
Splunk Enterprise is one of the supported instance types.
Splunk Express is not mentioned as a supported type.
Splunk Hadoop is not mentioned as a supported type.

questions.question: Why might you not see SIEM events immediately after connecting to a new Panther instance?

options.text: You do not have admin privileges in the partner tool
options.explanation: Admin privileges are required for setup, but the delay is related to the instance readiness.

options.text: It can take up to 10 minutes for the instance to be fully ready
options.explanation: When a new Panther instance is created, it can take up to 10 minutes for it to be fully ready to receive events.

options.text: The HMAC authentication key is incorrect
options.explanation: An incorrect key would result in connection failure, not just a delay.

options.text: The event is labeled differently in the SIEM
options.explanation: While events might be labeled differently, the primary reason for an immediate lack of events is instance readiness.

questions.question: How do you disconnect a DLP partner integration like Nightfall AI?

options.text: Select the tile and click Remove
options.explanation: You must select the specific menu option to disconnect.

options.text: Select ••• beside the Nightfall integration → Disconnect
options.explanation: The disconnect process involves selecting the three dots (•••) beside the integration tile and choosing Disconnect.

options.text: Go to the Nightfall application and remove the workspace
options.explanation: While the partner app needs updating, the primary disconnect step is done within Notion.

options.text: Delete the integration from the Connections list
options.explanation: The specific instruction is to select the three dots beside the tile.
